mission.title: Install and Configure Artifact Hub on Kubernetes
mission.description: Artifact Hub is a web-based application that enables finding, installing, and publishing Cloud Native packages. You would install it to streamline the discovery and management of various artifacts used in CNCF projects.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Artifact Hub Helm repository to your Helm configuration. This allows you to install the Artifact Hub chart using Helm.
```bash
helm repo add artifact-hub https://artifacthub.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the Artifact Hub chart
steps.description:
Install the Artifact Hub chart in a new namespace called 'artifact-hub'. This command will deploy the application with the specified version.
```bash
helm install artifact-hub artifact-hub/artifact-hub --version 1.22.0 --namespace artifact-hub --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the pods are running correctly in the 'artifact-hub' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n artifact-hub
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access the application
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access the Artifact Hub web interface locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/artifact-hub 8080:80 -n artifact-hub
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure the logging level for Artifact Hub by updating the values. You can set the log level to 'debug' for more verbose output.
```bash
helm upgrade artifact-hub artifact-hub/artifact-hub --set log.level=debug -n artifact-hub
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Artifact Hub release from your Kubernetes cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall artifact-hub --namespace artifact-hub
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and other resources associated with Artifact Hub.
```bash
kubectl delete crd artifacthubrepositories.artifacthub.io
kubectl delete crd artifacthubpackages.artifacthub.io
```

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'artifact-hub' namespace to clean up any remaining resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace artifact-hub
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, you may want to back up the current configuration and data.
```bash
helm get values artifact-hub --namespace artifact-hub > artifact-hub-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of the Artifact Hub chart.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Artifact Hub installation
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Artifact Hub release to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade artifact-hub artifact-hub/artifact-hub --version 1.22.0 --namespace artifact-hub
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the pods to confirm that the upgrade was successful.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n artifact-hub
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
If the pods are stuck in Pending, check for insufficient resources in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n artifact-hub
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the service, ensure the port-forward command is running and check the service status.
```bash
kubectl get svc -n artifact-hub
```

troubleshooting.title: Database connection issues
troubleshooting.description:
If there are errors related to database connectivity, verify the database configuration in the values.yaml and ensure the PostgreSQL service is running.
```bash
kubectl logs <artifact-hub-pod-name> -n artifact-hub
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Artifact Hub will result in a running instance accessible via port 8080 on your local machine. You should see the Artifact Hub interface when navigating to http://localhost:8080.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add artifact-hub https://artifacthub.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm upgrade artifact-hub artifact-hub/artifact-hub --set log.level=debug -n artifact-hub

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed.
